_about: Long-generation degradation of 4-bit KV quant (asym-NF4 vs fp16). Distinct from the GQA codebook collapse: this is generation-length-driven and affects MHA and GQA models alike. Same harness (tq_paper_lb_shard.py), LongBench, full 200 unless noted.

per_task_gap_qwen2.5-7b._note: asym-NF4 gap to fp16 tracks max_new_tokens, NOT task type. samsum is summarization but short-gen -> fine; gov_report/multi_news are the only 512-gen tasks -> large gap.

not_gqa_specific._note: The long-gen gap appears on Llama-2-7B too (error-tolerant MHA), comparable to Qwen -> general, not GQA-driven.

drift_example_gov_report_qwen_idx0.fp16_head: ### Summary  This report examines multiyear procurement (MYP) and block buy contracting (BBC)...
drift_example_gov_report_qwen_idx0.fp16_tail: ...Continued use of MYP and BBC could help DOD maximize value from its procurement budget...
drift_example_gov_report_qwen_idx0.asym_nf4_head: ### Summary  This report examines the use of Multiyear Procurement (MYP) and Block Buy Contracting (BBC)...
drift_example_gov_report_qwen_idx0.asym_nf4_tail: ...for ( (,. real ( ( ( ( the: a for a the like the for and aL of and M and DD and and under and-for to...

mechanism: Each decode step reads the quantized prefill with a small residual key error; over hundreds of steps the model conditions on its own drifting output and the error compounds -> faithful head, degenerate tail (token soup), often no EOS so it runs to max length. Remedy is orthogonal to the codebook: larger fp16 hot window or >=5-bit keys for long-output workloads.
max_gen_ablation_qwen_gov_report: IN PROGRESS (mg 64/128/256 x fp16/asym-NF4) — expected: gap grows with max_gen.
